perturbation.original_text: AMENDMENT NO. 3 TO STREMICK'S HERITAGE FOODS, LLC and PREMIER NUTRITION CORPORATION MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: AMENDMENT NO. 3 TO STREMICK'S HERITAGE FOODS, LLC and PREMIER NUTRITION CORPORATION MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Supplier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Supplier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Premier' to 'Supplier' creates confusion regarding the parties involved. While Premier is defined as Premier Nutrition Corporation, using 'Supplier' introduces ambiguity as to whether 'Supplier' is synonymous with 'Premier Nutrition Corporation' or refers to a different entity altogether. This leads to an in-text contradiction since the entire contract refers to 'Premier' as one of the parties. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes over which entity is bound by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AMENDMENT NO. 3 TO STREMICK'S HERITAGE FOODS, LLC and PREMIER NUTRITION CORPORATION MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Supplier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Supplier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement"). Heritage and Premier are each referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Premier Nutrition Corporation' with 'Supplier' while the contradicted text defines both 'Heritage' and 'Premier' as 'Parties'. By introducing 'Supplier' and potentially creating ambiguity as to whether 'Supplier' is synonymous with 'Premier Nutrition Corporation', it creates a contradiction within the document, specifically regarding who the parties of the agreement are. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction since the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage and Premier are each referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." WHEREAS, Heritage and Premier entered into the Agreement; WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage and the Vendor are each referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." WHEREAS, Heritage and Premier entered into the Agreement; WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Premier' with 'the Vendor' creates confusion because 'Premier' is the entity with whom Heritage originally entered into the agreement. Using 'Vendor' without defining it makes it unclear whether the 'Vendor' and 'Premier' are the same entity. The text then contradicts itself by stating that 'Heritage and Premier entered into the agreement'. This raises doubt about the validity of the amendment and the parties involved.
perturbation.location: Following the definition of the parties
perturbation.contradicted_location: Following the definition of the parties
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage and the Vendor are each referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." WHEREAS, Heritage and Premier entered into the Agreement; WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Premier' with 'the Vendor' in the definition of 'Party,' while the 'WHEREAS' clause still refers to 'Heritage and Premier' entering the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction because the contract now states two different entities (Premier and the Vendor) are parties to the original agreement, creating confusion. The Contradicted text is: 'Heritage and the Vendor are each referred to herein as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." WHEREAS, Heritage and Premier entered into the Agreement; WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.' The change means that it is unclear whether premier and the vendor are the same entity. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where the contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Amendment to be signed by their respective duly authorized representatives as of the Amendment Effective Date. Premier Nutrition Corporation Stremick's Heritage Foods, LLC. By: /s/ Darcy Davenport By: /s/ Sam Stremick Name: Darcy Davenport Name: Sam Stremick Title: President Title: President Source: BELLRING BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/20/2019
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Amendment to be signed by their respective duly authorized representatives as of the Amendment Effective Date. Nutrition Provider Stremick's Heritage Foods, LLC. By: /s/ Darcy Davenport By: /s/ Sam Stremick Name: Darcy Davenport Name: Sam Stremick Title: President Title: President Source: BELLRING BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/20/2019
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Premier Nutrition Corporation' to 'Nutrition Provider' in the signature block, without defining 'Nutrition Provider' elsewhere, creates ambiguity. The initial part of the agreement defines 'Premier Nutrition Corporation' as one of the parties. Since 'Nutrition Provider' is not defined, it's unclear if it is the same entity as Premier Nutrition Corporation. This raises the question of whether the signature is legally binding, creating an in-text contradiction because the signature block no longer aligns with the defined parties to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Amendment to be signed by their respective duly authorized representatives as of the Amendment Effective Date. Nutrition Provider Stremick's Heritage Foods, LLC. By: /s/ Darcy Davenport By: /s/ Sam Stremick Name: Darcy Davenport Name: Sam Stremick Title: President Title: President Source: BELLRING BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/20/2019
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Premier Nutrition Corporation' with 'Nutrition Provider' in the signature block. The explanation states that 'Nutrition Provider' is not defined elsewhere in the document, creating ambiguity as to whether it refers to the same entity as 'Premier Nutrition Corporation,' which is defined. Because the change introduces a new, undefined term in a critical part of the agreement (the signature block), it creates a clear contradiction with the document's original intent and defined parties, leading to uncertainty about the signature's validity. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, in this case, the obligation of the stated party to be bound.
